records.population: People in Australia with cardiovascular disease
verifiedBoundaries: Existing heart-failure fluid advice can modify generic hydration advice
Cold and flu medicines should be checked against existing medicines

records.population: Australian consumers considering cold, influenza or COVID-19 medicines
verifiedBoundaries: Severe heart disease and inadequately controlled high blood pressure are relevant decongestant safety contexts
NSAIDs may interact with cardiovascular medicines
Cold and flu combinations can create accidental duplication

records.population: Australian medicine sponsors and consumers reading compliant labels
verifiedBoundaries: Relevant examples require a doctor or pharmacist check for high blood pressure or heart problems
The document governs labels rather than clinical treatment

records.population: People in Australia with cardiac disease or other specified medical risk conditions
verifiedBoundaries: Cardiac disease is a specified medical-risk category
Individual product selection remains with the provider
